sql-data-management.title: SQL Data Management
sql-data-management.description: Move from reading data to managing it. Add approved rows, correct and remove records with precise conditions, design tables with useful constraints, connect related data, and complete a production-style SQLite capstone.
prerequisites: Multi-Table SQL & Joins, or equivalent experience reading relational schemas and following primary-key and foreign-key relationships.

sql-data-management-module-0-safe-insert.title: Add Rows Deliberately
sql-data-management-module-0-safe-insert.description: Move from reading existing data to adding approved rows with explicit columns, intentional NULL values, defaults, and controlled batches.
outcomes: Distinguish read-only queries from statements that change stored data.
Use a preview-change-verify workflow for deliberate mutations.
Write INSERT statements with explicit column lists.
Choose intentionally among literal values, SQL NULL, and table defaults.
Insert several approved rows in one controlled statement.
why: Move from reading existing data to adding approved rows with explicit columns, intentional NULL values, defaults, and controlled batches.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

sql-data-management-module-1-controlled-.title: Change and Remove Rows Safely
sql-data-management-module-1-controlled-.description: Preview target rows, apply precise UPDATE and DELETE statements, and verify that unrelated records remain unchanged.
outcomes: Preview the exact rows an UPDATE or DELETE will target.
Update one row with a precise key-based condition.
Update multiple columns or a controlled row set without broadening scope.
Delete only records confirmed by an explicit predicate.
Verify changed, deleted, and preserved rows after each mutation.
why: Preview target rows, apply precise UPDATE and DELETE statements, and verify that unrelated records remain unchanged.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

sql-data-management-module-2-table-creat.title: Build Tables with Guardrails
sql-data-management-module-2-table-creat.description: Create tables from a blank database and add keys, required values, defaults, uniqueness, and foreign-key relationships.
outcomes: Create a table with deliberate column names and SQLite data types.
Use PRIMARY KEY and NOT NULL to establish identity and required values.
Use UNIQUE and DEFAULT to protect business rules and routine values.
Declare a FOREIGN KEY that references a compatible parent key.
Inspect sqlite_master to verify the resulting table definition.
why: Create tables from a blank database and add keys, required values, defaults, uniqueness, and foreign-key relationships.
The work in this module becomes part of the next cumulative report or project.

sql-data-management-module-3-final-capst.title: Final Capstone
sql-data-management-module-3-final-capst.description: Design, load, correct, clean, and verify a small relational inventory database through one cumulative launch brief.
outcomes: Create two related tables with appropriate keys and constraints.
Insert approved parent and child rows with explicit column lists.
Use NULL and DEFAULT intentionally during data loading.
Apply a precise UPDATE and DELETE with required WHERE clauses.
Verify the final schema and data state without adding a new SQL concept.
why: Design, load, correct, clean, and verify a small relational inventory database through one cumulative launch brief.
The final work demonstrates the complete course workflow.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: A Safe Mutation Workflow
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Move from reading data to changing it safely through a repeatable preview, change, and verification workflow.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Values, NULL, and Defaults
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Control what a new row stores when a value is present, intentionally missing, or supplied by the table.
bullets: Adding Several Rows in One Statement

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Module Project
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Build a complete intake script while carrying every approved insert forward.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Target Before You Change
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Use familiar query conditions as a safety contract before the same condition controls a mutation.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Controlled Set Changes
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Change a justified set of rows or remove a confirmed record while keeping the mutation boundary explicit.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Module Project
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Carry a reviewed preview, two updates, and two deletes through one audited cleanup script.
bullets: Module 1 Project: Inventory Correction and Cleanup

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Table Structure and Identity
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Turn a blank database into a named table with clear columns, types, and required identifiers.
bullets: PRIMARY KEY and NOT NULL Constraints

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Defaults, Uniqueness, and Relationships
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Add rules that prevent duplicate business identifiers, supply routine values, and connect child rows to parent records.

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Module Project
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Build two related tables by replacing each earlier schema with a complete stronger definition.
bullets: Module 2 Project: Warehouse Stock Ledger Schema

sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.title: Final Capstone
sql-data-management-sql-data-management-.description: Create, load, correct, and clean a small relational inventory database from one production-style brief.
